global_cues.us_markets: Risk-on Friday close: S&P 500 +0.29% to record 7,230.12 (first close above 7,200); Nasdaq +0.89% to record 25,114.44 (above 25K for first time) on AI-trade tailwind and Apple rally; Dow lagged -0.31% to 49,499.27 weighed by oil-sensitive industrials. April was the best month for stocks since 2020. Positive read-through for Indian IT (TCS, INFY, HCLTECH, WIPRO, TECHM, LTIM) and Apple suppliers (DIXON).
global_cues.european_markets: May 1 was Workers' Day — most European bourses closed (DAX, CAC, etc. holiday). London FTSE 100 traded marginally down -0.04%. Prior session (Apr 30): DAX +0.68% recovery, CAC -0.53%. No fresh European lead, GIFT Nifty and US prints dominate the cue stack.
global_cues.asian_markets: Asia Monday open watched after weekend cooling of crude (Brent eased from ~$120 peak to $107-111). Apr 30 prints: Hang Seng +1.68% to 26,112 on dip-buying; Nikkei 225 ~59,513 (+0.38%). GIFT Nifty trading 24,229.5 (+0.34% vs prev close 24,208.5) — signals positive open for Nifty around the 24,000-24,250 zone. Tech-heavy Asia likely to take cues from Wall Street record close.

fii_dii_summary: FIIs heavy net SELLERS at -₹8,047.86 Cr on Apr 30 (last trading day before holiday); DIIs net BUYERS at +₹3,487.10 Cr — partial absorption only, leaving net institutional flow sharply negative going into May 4. FIIs offloaded ~₹70,100 Cr in April alone (10th straight month of net selling) and CY2026 cumulative outflows now ~₹2.4 lakh Cr. DII SIP flow (>₹20,000 Cr/month) cushioning but not arresting the trend; rupee weakness (~94.2) and elevated crude continue to deter foreign capital.
earnings_calendar: BHEL (Bharat Heavy Electricals) - May 4 (Q4 FY26 + dividend likely; Q3 had +189.8% PAT YoY at ₹390.4 Cr; defence/capex bellwether)
AMBUJACEM (Ambuja Cement) - May 4 (Q4 FY26 + dividend ₹3-5/sh expected; consensus ₹9,500-10,200 Cr rev, ₹850-1,000 Cr PAT, EBITDA/t ₹1,000-1,150, +12-15% volume YoY; Adani group)
TATATECH (Tata Technologies) - May 4 (Q4 FY26 + dividend likely; engineering/digital services)
JSL (Jindal Stainless) - May 4 (Q4 FY26 + dividend likely; stainless steel)
GODREJPROP (Godrej Properties) - May 4 (Q4 FY26 + dividend likely; real estate)
TATACHEM (Tata Chemicals) - May 4 (Q4 FY26 + dividend likely)
ATHERENERG (Ather Energy) - May 4 (Q4 + audited FY26 results; EV two-wheeler; Q3 had +50% YoY units to 68k, +53% YoY income)
MANAPPURAM (Manappuram Finance) - May 4 (Q4 FY26 + dividend likely; gold financier)
EXIDEIND (Exide Industries) - May 4 (Q4 FY26 + dividend likely; auto/industrial batteries)
PETRONET (Petronet LNG) - May 4 (Q4 FY26 + dividend likely; LNG/gas — read-through under crude shock)
SOBHA (Sobha) - May 4 (Q4 FY26 + dividend likely; real estate)
AARTIIND (Aarti Industries) - May 4 (Q4 FY26 + dividend likely; specialty chemicals)
QUESS (Quess Corp) - May 4 (Q4 FY26 + dividend likely)
JYOTHYLAB (Jyothy Labs) - May 4 (Q4 FY26 + dividend likely; FMCG/home care)
ORIENTHOT (Oriental Hotels) - May 4 (Q4 FY26 + dividend likely)
~45-47 NSE/BSE companies reporting on May 4 alone — heavy Q4 FY26 earnings flow
macro_events: Indian markets reopen after May 1 Maharashtra Day holiday; Apr 30 close was Sensex 76,913.50, Nifty 23,997.55, Bank Nifty 54,863.35 (-2.19% on the week)
RBI MPC (Apr 6-8) held repo at 5.25% with NEUTRAL stance for second straight meeting; FY27 GDP projected at 6.9%, CPI at 4.6% (Q1 4.0%, Q2 4.4%, Q3 5.2%, Q4 4.7%); next MPC Jun 3-5
US FOMC held rates unchanged Apr 29 amid oil shock; statement read as cautious — supportive for EM rates but USD strength persists
April HSBC India Manufacturing PMI accelerated to 55.9 (from 53.9 in March) — strong domestic activity signal; final services + composite PMI prints due this week
Iran war past 9 weeks (since Feb 28); Brent peaked ~$120 then partially retraced to $107-111 on tentative ceasefire/de-escalation talk; Strait of Hormuz disruption ongoing
Moody's cut India FY26-27 GDP to 6%; Goldman Sachs cut CY2026 to 5.9% — earnings revision cycle still skewed downward
Big Tech earnings (GOOGL, MSFT, AMZN, META) post-Apr 29 print drove US AI-trade rally to fresh records — overnight bid for Indian IT services and AI/data center plays
sector_themes: Capital Goods/Defence: BHEL Q4 today is the headline print — Q3 had +189.8% PAT YoY; positive read-through for L&T, BEL, HAL on order book momentum
Cement: AMBUJACEM Q4 expected strong (+12-15% volume YoY, EBITDA/t ₹1,000-1,150) — read-through to ULTRACEMCO, ACC, SHREECEM, DALBHARAT

EV/Auto: ATHERENERG first set of audited FY26 results post-IPO; EXIDEIND Q4 with dividend — positive 2W EV momentum supports TVSMOTOR, BAJAJ-AUTO EV plays; GST revisions (5-10% cut on autos) supportive for MARUTI, M&M, HEROMOTOCO
Real Estate: GODREJPROP and SOBHA reporting today — gauges urban housing demand under elevated rates; read-through to DLF, OBEROIRLTY, PRESTIGE, LODHA

Energy/Oil & Gas: PETRONET Q4 today amid LNG supply curtailment from Qatar — negative read for GAIL, IGL, MGL on gas volumes; OMC margin compression continues for IOC, BPCL, HPCL with Brent at $107-111; upstream ONGC, OIL beneficiaries
Specialty Chemicals: AARTIIND, TATACHEM Q4 today — sector under pressure from freight cost surge (capesize $9.80→$12.20/tonne) and Qatar LNG curtailment; PIDILITIND, SRF, NAVINFLUOR read-throughs
Pharma: WOCKPHARMA Q4 today; sector resilient with AI-driven export tailwinds offset by 15-30% freight cost surge — SUNPHARMA, DRREDDY, CIPLA, LUPIN positioning constructive

Banking: Bank Nifty down -2.19% last week — PSU banks (CANBK, UNIONBANK, BANKBARODA, PNB, SBIN) under pressure on RBI ECL framework + crude/INR macro stress; private banks HDFCBANK, ICICIBANK, AXISBANK, KOTAKBANK relatively defensive

IT Services: US AI-trade record close (Nasdaq +0.89%) supportive of TCS, INFY, HCLTECH, WIPRO, TECHM, LTIM despite ongoing structural headcount compression; mid-cap IT (PERSISTENT, COFORGE, MPHASIS) outperforming
Metals/Steel: JSL (stainless) Q4 today; freight cost pressure on landed met coal/iron ore for TATASTEEL, JSWSTEEL, JINDALSTEL, SAIL; NATIONALUM, HINDALCO read-throughs
FMCG/Consumer: JYOTHYLAB Q4 today — rural demand thesis under test; rupee weakness pressures palm oil/crude derivatives input costs for HINDUNILVR, MARICO, DABUR, GODREJCP
NBFC/Gold: MANAPPURAM Q4 today; gold financiers benefiting from elevated bullion and rural credit demand — read-through to MUTHOOTFIN, CHOLAFIN, BAJFINANCE
risk_flags: Brent crude at $107-111/bbl after peaking ~$120 — every $10 rise adds 20-25bps to India CPI and widens current account deficit by 40-50bps; Strait of Hormuz disruption persists
USD/INR around 94.2 vs ~85.6 a year ago — among worst-performing EM currencies; rupee depreciation imports inflation and pressures FII flows further
FII outflows accelerating: -₹8,047.86 Cr on Apr 30 alone, ~₹70,100 Cr in April (10th straight month), CY2026 cumulative ~₹2.4 lakh Cr — DII +₹3,487 Cr Apr 30 only partial offset
US-Iran war past 9 weeks; 220,000+ Indian nationals repatriated from Gulf/Iran — Gulf remittances (>1/3 of India total) at risk, downstream pressure on Kerala/AP banks and consumption
Bank Nifty -2.19% last week on PSU bank stress (RBI ECL framework) plus liquidity tightness — sector remains a tape-anchor risk
GDP downgrade stack: Moody's 6%, Goldman 5.9% for CY2026 — earnings cuts skewed to cyclicals/financials; consensus FY27 EPS revisions still trending negative
Heavy Q4 print day (45-47 firms): single-stock event risk concentrated — BHEL, AMBUJACEM, TATATECH, GODREJPROP, PETRONET, EXIDEIND misses could spill into respective sectors
Nifty consolidating in 23,800-24,300 range — break below 23,800 opens room toward 23,500; resistance band 24,400-24,500 must clear for upside continuation
brief_summary: Constructive open expected (GIFT Nifty +0.34% at 24,229.5) on Wall Street's record close (S&P 7,230, Nasdaq 25,114) and softer crude ($107-111 vs ~$120 peak), but the rally faces three headwinds: heavy FII selling (-₹8,047 Cr on Apr 30, 10th straight month of outflows), USD/INR weakness near 94.2, and a 47-firm earnings tape (BHEL, AMBUJACEM, TATATECH, GODREJPROP, PETRONET, EXIDEIND, ATHERENERG, JSL the bellwethers). Trade tactically: Nifty range 23,800-24,300 with 24,400-24,500 the breakout trigger; favor IT services (US AI tailwind), cement on AMBUJACEM print, and capital goods/defence on BHEL — fade OMCs (IOC, BPCL, HPCL) and PSU banks (CANBK, UNIONBANK, BANKBARODA) on macro stress.
